body.title: Web server down — does not auto-bring-itself-up
body.description: Web server is not running and did not self-recover. Possible causes: (1) missing build artifacts, (2) missing startup trigger in daemon, (3) recovery path not wired. Discovery step: check if web server binary/dependencies exist, whether it has a boot entry, and whether any recovery/health-check daemon monitors it.
body.result:
RESOLVED + verified self-healing & reboot-durable.

ROOT CAUSE (three faults in the Ground Loop activation chain, none in the backend code):
1. Descriptor never deposited — config/ground_loop/web_server.yaml existed in the repo but was never copied into the live scan dir ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/ (only logs/ was there), so Ground Loop never knew about the web server.
2. Ground Loop daemon was not running and its systemd unit was never installed — nothing was supervising anything.
3. Two descriptor defects: start_cmd used a bare 'python3' (system python3 lacks starlette/uvicorn -> silent ImportError, hidden by proxy stderr=DEVNULL); backend_port was 8080 (SSL) but http_proxy does plain-HTTP passthrough -> must be 8082 (the port internal clients already use).

FIX:
- Corrected descriptor: venv interpreter in start_cmd + PYTHONPATH; backend_port 8082 (commit 7a02e973). Deposited to live scan dir.
- plugin_proxy now captures backend stdout/stderr to ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/logs/web_server.backend.log instead of DEVNULL (commit c2c150b4) — the swallowed ImportError is why this needed diagnosis.
- Added boot-durable user-systemd unit systemd/ground_loop.user.service; installed + enabled via systemctl --user (linger already on, no sudo) (commit ff... 'feat: Ground Loop user-systemd unit').

VERIFIED (all 200 through :8078 proxy): cold-start spawns backend; kill backend -> next request transparently respawns; /api/health -> 200. Three protection layers: backend crash->proxy respawn; daemon crash->systemd Restart=on-failure; reboot->systemctl --user+linger. Unit active+enabled.
